summary: Kenneth Suna discusses his personal stock investment performance since 2022, arguing that individual investors can outperform the S&P 500 through stock picking and conviction investing. The video contains no options trading strategies, premium collection tactics, or risk management principles relevant to Brian Norton's Premium Desk covered call and cash-secured put wheel strategy.
takeaways: Individual stock selection requires time and research effort to execute effectively
High-conviction positions should be averaged into over multiple years rather than bought once
Dollar-cost averaging into winning positions (Nvidia, Meta, Palantir) across multiple years outperforms static allocation
Accepting losses on some positions (Cava, Hims, Meta) is part of a diversified stock-picking approach
Speculative positions (QBTS, Joby Aviation) are included alongside core holdings in a mixed portfolio
title: Nobody can outperform the S&P 500?
